# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD for Python Application
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  build:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install pytest

      - name: Test with pytest
        run: |
          python -m pytest --cov=.

  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Production
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && !contains(github.ref, 'heads/main')
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code with dependencies
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          subdirectory: ${{ github.workspace }}/path/to/deploy

      - name: Deploy to Production (custom action required)
        uses: your-organization/your-action@master
